works.lesson: Copper on warm amber = editorial warmth without red aggression. Differentiated from griflan red. Warm temperature intentional throughout.
Cool violet canvas = creative depth. Vermillion accent avoids copper (Forge) and purple (AI). Dual-theme palette with dark/light rhythm. 5.2:1 contrast on accent.
4-neon system with category coding (cyan=PCs, magenta=perifericos, yellow=componentes, lime=CTA). Blue-tinted near-black canvas gives depth. Each neon has 3 glow intensities (sm/md/lg).
Monochromatic green-tinted near-black with warm stone accents. No chromatic accent -- color from photography only. First project with zero chromatic accent.
Cool blue near-black + burnt clay terracotta creates warm-in-cold tension perfect for minimal dark
Sage green accent on near-black creates organic calm. Sandstone #d4cabb as secondary warms the void.
Electric lime + hot coral on violet-black = playful energy. Three-accent system creates chromatic variety without chaos.

works.reaction: Warm amber void with aged brass -- single accent discipline
works.lesson: Warm amber near-black creates envelope of warmth. Brass accent at 6.8:1 contrast used sparingly. Differentiated from cool blue (#0a0a12) and sage (#0b0b0c) minimal palettes.

failures.reason: Purple gradient = #1 AI fingerprint. Never use.

references.lesson: Bold yellow accent on near-black. High contrast single chromatic accent. Minimal palette with maximum impact.
Pure B&W. Violates near-black rule but works through extreme contrast and typography as the only design tool. Exception that proves the rule.

knownGaps: Zero light-canvas palettes in working history. Massive blind spot for light-theme projects.
Only 1 warm palette (Forge), 1 cool (practice-10), rest neutral. Color temperature skill underdeveloped.

_meta.cleanReason: Audit V1: deduplicated artefakt-mov (was listed twice), moved reference palettes to separate 'references' section with actual lessons, added themeType field, added knownGaps, enriched reference lessons.
